legacy.raw_response:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-BOR-001",
      "summary": "Bill of Rights is a scaffold with no human-authored articles, leaving the constitutional scope undefined and all operations in default-permissive mode.",
      "mission_reference": "Bill of Rights scaffold status; MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights': 'Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.' MSP 'Purpose': 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules.'",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "The entire governance perimeter is missing. Arbiter cannot enforce scope, Nomos cannot evaluate against BoR, and no prohibited activities are defined. All organ actions are effectively unrestricted, negating the safety architecture of the DIO.",
      "evidence": "World state graph_structural.concept_counts_by_type includes 'bor_version': 1 (scaffold). BoR document explicitly marked 'scaffold — awaiting human authorship' with all articles undefined. Spine transitions show Arbiter and Nomos alive but enforcing nothing because the BoR is empty."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-MSP-002",
      "summary": "MSP contains no operational rules, constraints, or invariants, leaving Nomos evaluation permissive for all action proposals that pass the (empty) Arbiter scope gate.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP 'Rules': 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.' MSP 'Purpose': Nomos evaluates 'MSP + genome compliance' as second gate.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Without MSP rules, Nomos cannot reject any action proposal, effectively disabling the second authorization gate. Even if Arbiter later enforces BoR scope, actions within scope would have zero operational constraints (no safety rules, no invariants).",
      "evidence": "MSP document explicitly states no rules. World state graph_structural shows 'msp_version': 1 (version 1.0.0-seed) with no subsequent rule-bearing versions. No PEMs would be generated because nothing is blocked."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-GENOME-003",
      "summary": "No organ-specific genome documents exist to extend the MSP with organ-level operating rules and risk-tier constraints.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP 'Genome References': 'Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' Status: '_(none at seed)_'.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Organs such as Glia (autoheal), Spine, Nomos, and Arbiter have no tailored safety guardrails beyond the empty MSP. Even after MSP rules are created, organ-specific behaviors may require additional constraint layers to prevent unsafe autonomous actions.",
      "evidence": "MSP states no genome references at seed. World state shows many organs actively managing state (spine transitions for 28+ organs), but no corresponding genome documents are registered or referenced."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-GOALS-004",
      "summary": "No mission objectives or goals have been dispatched, leaving the DIO without purposeful direction aligned to the (absent) Bill of Rights.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP 'Purpose': 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives...' The operational constitution should generate/drive goals, but the world state shows none.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "The DIO is operating in a reactive, maintenance-only mode without pursuing any defined enterprise goals. Without a BoR, no objectives can be derived, stalling the system's higher-level function and leaving all organ activity unguided by the mission.",
      "evidence": "World state 'Recent Goals (last dispatched)' is an empty array. Radiant context and memory contain extensive operational logs (backups, dream cycles, renames) but no mission-level goal concepts, objectives, or action proposals targeting a BoR-derived goal."
    }
  ]
}

per-domain-constitutional-schema-validation-failed:gap-constitutional[0] schema violation: gap/target_state must NOT have fewer than 1 characters; gap/mission_ref must NOT have fewer than 1 characters; gap/evidence_refs must NOT have fewer than 1 items; gap/evidence_refs must contain at least 1 valid item(s)
raw_responses:
```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "gap_title": "Bill of Rights absent — no constitutional foundation for scope enforcement",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Articles I–VI (all scaffold-only; scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, and interpretation rules are all undefined)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 125,
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is the highest constitutional authority in the DIO. Arbiter (#190) verifies it by hash at every scope query, yet the document is entirely scaffold with all six articles marked 'awaiting human authorship.' This means there is no defined scope of enterprise, no enumeration of prohibited activities, no core identity statement, and no reservation of human authority. Every downstream governance mechanism — the MSP, Nomos adjudication, genome constraints, and Senate legislative function — operates without a constitutional perimeter. The system is running in a vacuum where 'scope creep' has no definition, prohibited actions cannot be identified, and human authority has no reserved domain.",
      "evidence": "BoR document shows all articles as '[To be defined by human principal]'. MSP §Relationship to the Bill of Rights states 'The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights. Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.' Arbiter references BoR by hash at every scope query, yet the hash is of an empty scaffold. The MSP declares 'Scope expansion requires a BoR amendment, which is human-only' — but there is no scope baseline to expand from.",
      "why_strategic": "Without the BoR, the entire Nomos two-gate compliance architecture (Arbiter scope gate + MSP/genome gate) is operating on an undefined perimeter. This is the constitutional equivalent of a null pointer dereference at the root of the governance stack. Every action the DIO takes is technically authorized because nothing is prohibited. Every organ boundary is advisory rather than enforced. The system cannot distinguish between legitimate platform evolution and unconstitutional scope expansion because 'constitutional' has no content."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "gap_title": "MSP operational constitution remains empty — Nomos operates in default-permissive mode",
      "grounded_in": "MSP §Rules ('The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules'), MSP §Genome References ('none at seed'), MSP §Purpose (translation from BoR to machine-actionable constraints is entirely latent)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 100,
      "description": "The MSP is designated as the operational constitution that translates the BoR into precise, machine-actionable objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules that Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication. In its current seed state, it contains zero rules, zero genome references, and zero safety constraints. Nomos's second compliance gate — the MSP + genome compliance gate — is therefore a no-op. Combined with the empty BoR (gap-001), the entire governance enforcement pipeline is structurally present but substantively hollow. The amendment protocol, PEM pipeline, review/rollout flow, and hash-chained versioning infrastructure are all in place and waiting for content.",
      "evidence": "MSP v1.0.0-seed explicitly states '(none)' under Rules and Genome References. Status is 'Active bootstrap scaffold.' The Nomos adjudication path is defined as '(1) Arbiter scope gate (BoR perimeter), (2) MSP + genome compliance gate' — gate 1 has no perimeter defined (gap-001), and gate 2 has no rules to evaluate. The amendment protocol describes a full PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout pipeline with HOM notifications and hash-verified append-only versioning, but this machinery has processed zero amendments because no APs can be meaningfully blocked when no rules exist.",
      "why_strategic": "The DIO has invested heavily in governance infrastructure (Nomos, Arbiter, Senate, PEM pipeline, HOM notifications, hash-verified MSP versioning) but has not yet populated the operational ruleset that gives it purpose. Every Action Proposal is authorized by default. The system is building organs, executing backups, running dream cycles, and managing context/memory without any operational guardrails. This is acceptable during bootstrap only if the bootstrap is genuinely temporary — but the evidence shows a platform with 28 organs, active development velocity, and operational complexity far exceeding what 'bootstrap scaffold' implies."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-003",
      "gap_title": "Dream cycle health degraded — autonomous memory consolidation is disabled, leaving context/memory formation ungoverned by MSP strategic objectives",
      "grounded_in": "MSP §Purpose (the MSP is meant to translate BoR into 'machine-actionable objectives' for system function; memory/context formation is a core cognitive function with no operational constraints), Radiant dream stats showing scheduled=disabled, health=never_run, last_dream=null",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 64,
      "description": "The Radiant dream cycle — responsible for context freshness triage (Phase 1, deterministic) and memory consolidation (Phase 2, LLM-driven) — is in a degraded state. Dream scheduling is explicitly disabled ('scheduled': 'disabled'), health tracking has never run ('health': 'never_run'), and there is no record of the last completed dream cycle. Phase 2 consolidation has repeatedly been deferred to interactive Claude Code sessions because MCP tools are unavailable in scheduled task sessions (as documented in cycles #12, #13, #15). The embedding coverage gap of 28% (27 of 96 memory blocks lack embeddings) further degrades semantic retrieval. The watchdog grace period is set to 36 hours (129,600,000 ms) but has never been checked. The strategic function of memory — providing coherent, consolidated, retrievable context for agent decision-making — is operating without governance, without an operational objective from the MSP, and without autonomous reliability.",
      "evidence": "radiant.stats shows { scheduled: 'disabled', last_dream: null, health: 'never_run', watchdog: { alert_count: 0, last_alert_at: null, last_check_at: null } }. embeddings coverage at 72% (69/96 with embeddings, 27 without). Dream cycle reports show repeated Phase 2 deferrals: 'deferred to next interactive Claude Code session' (cycles #12, #13) and MCP workaround pattern ('MCP tools not available in scheduled task session — used direct psql'). Context block avg_age_days of 31.8 for the primary entity (llm-ops) suggests consolidation is not keeping pace with accumulation. The 5 context blocks expiring within 48 hours out of 16 total may indicate triage is functional but consolidation is not.",
      "why_strategic": "Memory formation and consolidation are the cognitive backbone of the DIO — they determine what the system remembers, how it retrieves relevant history, and what context informs its decisions. An empty MSP means no strategic objectives govern what should be remembered, how memory should be prioritized, or what constitutes healthy memory hygiene. The dream cycle's degraded autonomous operation means the system's 'hippocampus' is dependent on human-initiated interactive sessions, creating a single point of human dependency in what should be an autonomous cognitive loop. This gap directly undermines the DIO's ability to function as a persistent, self-maintaining intelligence organism."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-004",
      "gap_title": "No MSP genome references defined — organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier constraints are absent",
      "grounded_in": "MSP §Genome References ('Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. — (none at seed)'), MSP §Rules (zero rules published means zero organ-specific constraints exist)",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 36,
      "description": "The MSP architecture envisions genome documents as extensions that provide organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. These would define, for example, what Glia (autoheal) is permitted to do autonomously vs. what requires human approval, what probabilistic services like Radiant's dreamer may modify, what deterministic services like Spine must guarantee, and what risk tiers apply to different categories of operation. The seed contains zero genome references. Combined with the empty MSP rules, there are no organ-level guardrails. The platform has 28 active organs (per the vision document), each with distinct risk profiles and operational domains, yet none have genome-encoded constraints.",
      "evidence": "MSP §Genome References explicitly states '(none at seed)'. The vision document in recent_memory (id: bab3c3b5) enumerates 28 active organs across 10 categories including deterministic services (Spine, Graphheight, SafeVault), probabilistic services (Radiant, Minder, Autoheal, Syntra), and governance organs (Nomos, Arbiter, Cerberus, Senate). Autoheal's fixer component (recent_memory id: 817a964c) has 6 safety guardrails implemented procedurally in bash, but these are not encoded as MSP genome constraints — they exist as script-level enforcement, not constitutional-level governance. The Glia organ (formerly Autoheal) can propose and execute code changes with human approval required, but this approval requirement is a procedural convention, not a genome-enforced constraint.",
      "why_strategic": "As the organ count grows and autonomous capabilities expand (classifier self-evolving knowledge base, fixer self-evolving fix-history), the gap between implemented safety mechanisms and constitutionally encoded constraints widens. Procedural safeguards in scripts are mutable by the same agents they constrain. Genome constraints would be enforced at the Nomos adjudication level, creating a separation of powers where operational code cannot override constitutional rules. Without genome references, each organ's safety boundaries exist only in its own implementation — a structural vulnerability as the system scales toward hosting tenant DIOs."
    }
  ]
}
```
